summary_one_line: Input must carry a fixed IPv6-header byte prefix that routes the packet through UDP/ICMP6 handlers whose handlers return a non-kErrorNone status.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A fixed multi-byte IPv6-header prefix at offsets 0x00-0x07 (W seeds share `78 66 65 5c 7[7-8] 00 10 11`, where 0x11 = kProtoUdp at the next-header byte) that lets HandlePayload reach the UDP/ICMP6 dispatch and have the chosen handler return a non-kErrorNone status so `if (error != kErrorNone)` takes the true branch at ip6.cpp:981.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: W seeds all carry the constant 8-byte header prefix (offsets 0x00-0x07 collapse to one or two values), routing the message into `case kProtoUdp` or `case kProtoIcmp6` and exclusively reaching the ICMP6 handler at line 972-973 and the UDP error-drop branch at line 966, which produces error != kErrorNone (winner takes T=10 at line 981, loser takes T=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: L seeds have widely varied bytes at offsets 0x00-0x07 (e.g. byte 0 spans 80/81/7f, byte 6 spans 64/29, byte 7 spans 00/29) so the IPv6 header is malformed before HandlePayload; the loser only reaches `case kProtoUdp` with successful UDP processing (error stays kErrorNone) and never exercises the ICMP6 case (line 972-973 is W-only [W] in the per-role overlay), so error remains kErrorNone and line 981 never takes the true branch in 10/10 loser trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the multi-byte equality CMPs that parse the IPv6 header (next-header/length fields), reads the literal protocol constants from the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, and substitutes them into the input in one mutation step. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they cannot perform input-to-state substitution and must rely on havoc mutation to randomly hit a multi-byte exact prefix, which is exponentially unlikely within budget. The asymmetry is between I2S-equipped fuzzers (cmplog, vpc) and the rest, exactly matching the [delta: I2S] axis of both decisive pairs.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is `if (error != kErrorNone)` at ip6.cpp:981, true side globally blocked.
evidence_trail.cited_locator: Location ip6.cpp:981:9, Globally blocked side: T

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs share the I2S technique delta and resolve every trial vs zero loser trials.
Pair 2 also has I2S delta, supporting single_feature classification with one shared mechanism axis.
Only the winner reaches the ICMP6 dispatch case in HandlePayload (lines 972-973 are W-only in the per-role coverage overlay).
The error-true side of the blocker is reached only by W; L never takes the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 981  T=10 F=20  T=0 F=20  if (error != kErrorNone)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: W reaches the UDP error-drop branch (line 966) nine times while L never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 966  T=9 F=20  T=0 F=20  if (error == kErrorDrop)

evidence_trail.claim: All five winner-resolving seeds share the leading byte 0x78 at offset 0 (10/10) while L bytes at offset 0 are 80/81/7f, evidencing a multi-byte fixed-prefix gate.
Offset 0x06 is 0x10 in 10/10 W seeds but 0x64/0x29 in L seeds — a constant byte the winner must match.
Offset 0x07 is 0x11 (= kProtoUdp = 17) in 9/10 W seeds but 0x00 in 9/10 L seeds — the next-header protocol field.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace mutator reads CmpLogObserver and substitutes one operand of the CMP into the input, enabling exact multi-byte matches in one mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S; only edge-counter feedback and havoc mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; its CMP_MAP only buckets by Hamming/prefix distance.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 8477 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0396cf0f608eb8a1` and observed one I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 21 of the 22-level ancestor chain. Tool output: `# I2S-floor signal: 1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 21.` This is the dash-row signal that under the current LibAFL fuzzbench build is exclusive to cmplog/vpc and characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness that gates a true branch on an 8-byte fixed IPv6-header-shaped equality CMP (where one operand is a runtime-loaded literal at the same address each call) and a downstream handler that returns a non-success error were built, and cmplog FAILED to resolve it while naive resolved it within the same budget, the I2SRandReplace attribution would be refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP site whose operands I2S substitutes is not directly localized in the prompt — the chain from offset 0x06=0x10 to which OpenThread header field is parsed could be in the harness wrapper rather than in HandlePayload's call chain, so the 'multi-byte prefix' description is the right shape but the bytes may correspond to multiple smaller CMPs upstream.
